title: The Fall and Rise of Billy Idol
content:
With his defiant sneer and bleached-blond spikes, Billy Idol has been the face of punk rock for nearly fifty years. As a teenager in London, he traveled in a pack with other future music legends, wearing sliced-up T-shirts and leather pants. In 1976, at the young age of twenty, he started his own punk band inspired by the legendary Sex Pistols. Born William Broad, he took his famous stage name from a teacher's report-card complaint that 'William is idle.' He later admitted that school had never been his strong suit due to an undiagnosed attention disorder.

After embarking on a solo career, Idol scored a massive string of iconic hits, including 'Rebel Yell' and 'White Wedding.' His 1981 breakthrough, 'Dancing With Myself,' proved its undeniable power when he watched a New York City club crowd rush the dance floor. Seeing the wild reaction to his music cemented his desire to dedicate his life to performing. Unfortunately, the intense pressures of fame soon derailed him, leading to severe struggles with drugs and alcohol. He later reflected on how he had everything he ever wanted, only to nearly burn it all down.

Despite these profound struggles, Billy Idol ultimately survived his darkest days and maintained his legendary rock status. Even at seventy years old, he continues to play live shows with the same raw energy that defined his youth. He still resides in the sprawling Hollywood Hills compound that he originally purchased in 1987. Interestingly, he discovered early on that the property's previous owner had used the lush gardens for unsavory private activities. Today, however, the home serves as a peaceful sanctuary for a punk rock survivor who outlasted his wildest era.
